mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuation multiples and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates and FEDFUNDS remains far below CPI growth, real policy rates stay negative, sustaining easy liquidity conditions that support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the signal does not deliver positive 60-day forward returns in triggered cases, or if returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of unemployment-rise triggers, or if the signal's 20-day hit rate is indistinguishable from random over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger retail spending supported by payroll growth, which raises near-term revenue expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-upturn condition fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if either proxy alone explains the effect better than the pair.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which confirms a strengthening earnings backdrop and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO up months are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the short-horizon Rank IC is below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and slower downstream consumption, which should depress equity expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if below-average HOUST readings are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior 3-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating faster than firms are hiring, which should reduce earnings visibility and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration trigger does not lead to negative 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the interaction term is insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 with stable or falling UNRATE indicates broad output expansion without worsening labor stress, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises sequentially while UNRATE does not increase by more than 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-up, unemployment-stable episodes fail to outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if the signal becomes negative in any 3-year rolling window.
